feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Community
feed.subtitle: SAP Community blog posts in SAP Community

entry.title: Bem vindo ao grupo da Comunidade SAP do PORTO (Portugal)
entry.content:
Este espaço foi criado para reunir profissionais, entusiastas e empresas que utilizam o SAP, promovendo um ambiente de troca de conhecimentos e experiências. Nosso objetivo é fortalecer a rede de contatos entre os membros da comunidade, facilitar o acesso a informações relevantes e atualizações sobre o SAP, e destacar as iniciativas e histórias de sucesso locais.

O Porto, uma das cidades mais bonitas de Portugal, oferece uma experiência única que combina história, cultura, gastronomia e belezas naturais. Situa-se na região Norte de Portugal. Aqui estão algumas razões pelas quais você deve visitar o Porto:

  • Centro Histórico: A Ribeira, classificada como Patrimônio Mundial pela UNESCO, encanta com suas ruas estreitas, casas coloridas e igrejas barrocas.
  • Livraria Lello: Considerada uma das mais bonitas do mundo, é um verdadeiro paraíso para os amantes de livros e de arquitetura.
  • Vinho do Porto: Desfrute de degustações em caves tradicionais ao longo do rio Douro. Uma experiência imperdível para os apreciadores de vinhos.
  • Culinária Local: Delicie-se com pratos típicos como a francesinha, bacalhau à Gomes de Sá e os tradicionais petiscos em tascas locais.
  • Rio Douro: Aproveite um passeio de barco pelo rio Douro, oferecendo vistas deslumbrantes das margens e das vinhas.
  • Jardins e Parques: Explore o Parque da Cidade e os jardins do Palácio de Cristal, perfeitos para momentos de relaxamento e piqueniques.
  • Pontes Icônicas: Admire a Ponte Dom Luís I, projetada por um discípulo de Gustave Eiffel, uma maravilha da engenharia e um símbolo da cidade.
  • Museus e Galerias: Visite o Museu de Arte Contemporânea de Serralves e a Casa da Música para uma imersão no mundo da arte e da música.
  • Pessoas Acolhedoras: Os portuenses são conhecidos por sua simpatia e hospitalidade, fazendo você se sentir em casa desde o primeiro momento.
  • Ambiente Vibrante: A cidade oferece uma mistura única de tradição e modernidade, com uma vida noturna animada, festivais e eventos culturais.

Não faltam motivos para se associar a este grupo, participar dos nossos eventos no PORTO e conhecer esta cidade cosmopolita.

Neste blog, você encontrará uma variedade de conteúdos, desde artigos técnicos e tutoriais até notícias sobre eventos e formações. Queremos que este seja um ponto de encontro virtual onde todos possam aprender, compartilhar e crescer juntos.

Junte-se a nós e descubra como a Comunidade SAP PORTO pode ajudar-te a alcançar novos patamares na tua carreira e em teus projetos!

entry.title: SAP Inside Track PORTO 2024
entry.content:
SAP Inside Track PORTO will have it first edition in PORTO (Portugal) on September 28, 2024.

Look here in the SAP Community Event Calendar.

The event is present in several countries around the world. Its organizers are active members of the SAP Community with the aim of sharing knowledge and strengthening professionals among the participants.

The event has been in existence for over 15 years and has been held in several cities around the world.

Learn more about how to hold your SAP Inside Track event in your city through this LINK.

Inscription:

Includes:

  • Access to the event;
  • Access to all the lectures;
  • Candidates can be received to participate as a volunteer of the event;
  • Admissions to the event, here in the Link 

 

Speaker:

Take this opportunity to share your knowledge and help us make this event even better.

Applications for lectures can be made at this LINK

    • A proposal with the title theme and a synopsis should be sent with the subject SITPOR
    • The lecture time is a maximum of 40 minutes.
    • The last day for submitting proposals is  Aug  25, 2024.
    • Wait for the contact of our team until Aug 30 with confirmation.

Event’s place

The Lodge Hotel

Rua Serpa Pinto, 60 - 4400-307 V. N. Gaia – Porto

On September 28 2024  (Saturday)

 

Agenda

8 am – Reception

9 am to 12 pm – Presentations / lectures

12h to 13:30 – Lunch

14h to 17:30 – Lectures

17:30 to 18:00 – Closing session

Speakers:

Lecture NameSpeaker
SAP Project: Technology or business?António Andrade
SAP Build Work Zone Content Federation deep diveFabio Pagoti
Global eCompliance with SAP Document and Reporting ComplianceFelipe Borges
SAP Implementation Change Management how to manage organizations and peopleMarta Mota
SAP in Industry 4.0Tiago Fernandes
Gen AIBruno Costa
The era of Digitalization and SustainabilityFilipe Silva
SAP IS RetailAndré Margi

 

Organization Team

Manuel Robalinho ( @MRobalinho )
Arnaldo Calçada
Pedro Rodrigues
Pedro Marques
Pedro Vieira
Fátima Linhares
Matheus Brasil  
António Elias
 
Aditional informations and Contacts:
Email: SAPInsideTrackPorto@gmail.com

entry.title: Clean Core, Clean Code and Test Driven Development: ABAP CodeRetreat in Bern
entry.content:
I was happy to be part of the ABAP CodeRetreat Event in Bern.

(https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-blog-posts/abap-coderetreat-we-re-back-and-better-than-ever/ba-p/13560920)

The event took place on a saturday at the swisscom offices in Bern. swisscom is famous as a telco company, but swisscom is also a sap consulting company with over 400 sap experts in Switzerland.

the participants traveled to Bern from different sap customers and partners in italy, germany, austria and Switzerland, and the exchange with sap community members was one of the highlights of the event.

Damir and Martin did a very professional presentation about the impact and benefits of clean code and abap testing and the methodology around TDD Test Driven Development, so everyone was highly motivated to try it 'hands on' in different exercises.

 

Here are my personal Key Take-Aways:

- Finding Bugs early and automated with Unit Tests are the cheapest way, all Following up Tests like Integration Tests and UAT are way more effort

- clean code means having less lines of code and less maintenance costs for custom developments. 

- clean code is an ongoing task, getting better every day

- clean core will increase the need for proper unit tests

- good skills with Eclipse and ADT are essential for efficient development with clean core and unit test classes (lots of quick-fixes and shortcuts), it is not efficient with SE80

 - wow effect by showing CoPilot4Eclipse, which can also generate ABAP Code with AI #abapisnotdead

- pair programming was fun and gave a lot of additional learnings around sap and abap

 

Many thanks to everyone who helped making this event a success. I definitely learned a lot, and of course this methodology requires continuous training, as everything around developers life. The event closed at 15.00, just in time to watch Switzerlands first game at the Euro2024 😄

entry.title: Check Out the Latest Groups and Topic Pages!
entry.content:
Since our last post about new SAP Community areas, we've added many new groups and topic pages.

If you're looking to connect with members in your location or learn more about specific SAP offerings and programs, you may want to explore these new groups and pages.

In the Americas, we added two location groups for Brazil: Fortaleza and Recife. And speaking of Brazil, we're happy to report that the previously retired São Paulo group is back and more vibrant than ever!

Elsewhere in the world, we added a group for Almaty, Kazakhstan, to the Asia Pacific section, and for Europe, we've launched groups for Dortmund, Germany, Oslo, Norway, Porto, Portugal, and Tbilisi, Georgia.

Location groups give members the opportunity to blog and join discussions about their cities, so they can connect with others close to home. Several of these groups even have their own event boards, making it easier for members to meet up with colleagues in their regions.

The SAP SuccessFactors groups also give members options to connect with users from their locations (although these groups are open to customers only). For example, we recently launched SAP SuccessFactors HCM Oklahoma User Group. We also launched SAP SuccessFactors NS2 CRE User Group for SAP National Security Services customers from the commercial regulated environment.

Again, those SAP SuccessFactors groups aren't open to all members, but another new group (the latest in Influence and Feedback Groups) is: SAP MaxAttention. This group provides an open channel dedicated to this program of services.

In addition to the groups mentioned above, we rolled out several new topic pages recently. These pages provide members with in-depth information about specific topics, and we've expanded this part of the site with pages for:

  • Customer Influence and Adoption: Learn how to influence SAP software development, adopt new innovations early on, and request specific enhancements or features.
  • SAP Accessibility: Get information about the SAP team and program championing inclusivity in software design and engineering.
  • SAP Product Lifecycle Management: Access this one-stop shop to learn about the features and functions of SAP PLM -- and to enhance your knowledge by connecting with experts.
  • SAP S/4HANA for Microsoft Teams: Find out how to improve your business decisions by combining information from your SAP S/4HANA Cloud system of record with expertise from your team members connected using Microsoft Teams.
  • User Research at SAP: Discover how SAP conducts user research studies to apply actionable user insights and create experiences that make SAP users more effective at their jobs.

We have even more groups in the works, so keep an eye on What's New -- particularly on the SAP Community Voice entries, as we're moving our newsletter fully to What's New, and in future, we'll use SAP Community Voice posts to promote the best and latest content on our platform.

entry.title: Lead with Resilience
entry.content:
The past few years have been a rollercoaster ride for both individuals and businesses, with the COVID-19 pandemic bringing unprecedented challenges and changes. One group that has faced particularly difficult times is people managers, who have had to navigate the new normal while leading their teams through uncharted territory.

For those who became managers during the COVID years, the challenges have been even more daunting. Adapting to remote work, managing teams virtually, dealing with uncertainty, and the constant changes due to the business impact have taken a toll on the resilience of many leaders. As a result, there is a growing need to address resilience in people managers to ensure they can lead effectively in challenging times.

In response to this growing need, a program called "Lead with Resilience" has been designed in India by the regional Coaching Ambassadors in collaboration with Shwetha Mohanty, Head of HR, SAP in India. This coaching-centric program aims to enable managers to lead with resilience, helping them bounce back from setbacks, respond to adversity with strength and speed, adapt to disruptive changes, and sustain their energy under pressure.

 

 

 

 

 

 

The program provides an opportunity for managers to reflect upon their experiences and identify and address blind spots that may be hindering their ability to lead with resilience. It encourages a solution-based thought process when dealing with challenges, and helps develop emotional resilience to perceive challenges without giving in to negative sentiments.

The Lead with Resilience program includes:
1. 6 coaching sessions, where participants will work with experienced coaches to develop their resilience as leaders.
2. Hot seat sessions where senior leaders share their personal stories of resilience, providing valuable insights and inspiration.
3. Experiential Workshops such as Coachable Moments, Ready for Difficult Conversations, and Resilience Circles offer practical tools and techniques for building resilience.
4. Shadowing sessions allow participants to observe their coaches in team-specific meetings, providing a unique learning opportunity.
5. Networking lunches, providing a chance to connect with leaders, coaches, and peers, fostering a supportive and collaborative environment.

To ensure the effectiveness of the program, before and after ripple surveys are conducted to assess the impact of the program, and a leaderboard encourages participants to actively engage and participate.

The minimum requirements to complete the program include 4 coaching sessions, 2 reflective journals, an SDI assessment, 2 hot seat sessions, and 2 workshops.

In conclusion, the Lead with Resilience program offers a comprehensive and practical approach to developing resilience in people managers. In a time when resilience is more important than ever, this program provides the necessary

entry.title: Sharing Power, Doubling Success - That's Co-Leadership @ SAP! 🚀
entry.content:
Co-Leadership as a Pathway to New Work?

Co-Leadership is the new buzzword echoing through office corridors. But what exactly does it mean? Is it just a temporary fad, or does it represent the dawn of a new work era as part of the New Work movement? It's clear to all of us that the labor market has undergone significant changes in recent years. Due to a shortage of skilled labor, which complicates the recruitment of new talent, companies are being forced to rethink their strategies. Factors such as employer branding and diversity are increasingly coming to the fore. At the same time, the needs of employees have also changed.

Remote working is becoming increasingly important for many employees - whether it's to achieve a better work-life balance through sufficient free time, or to better reconcile work and family life. Women, who often shoulder the main responsibility for unpaid care work and consequently work part-time, are often deprived of the opportunity to take on higher and managerial roles. This is where the current trend of co-leadership comes in - a form of job-sharing that opens up new opportunities.

Co-leadership is being implemented by a number of renowned companies, including the online fashion retailer Zalando, Bosch.IO - a subsidiary of Bosch, the building management service provider Apleona, as well as local institutions such as Canon Switzerland and the Zürcher Kantonalbank. As of 2023, SAP Switzerland has also been led by Sabrina Storck and Thomas Schreitmüller as Co-Managing Directors.

 

"Co-leadership is just for part-time working women...?!"

Let's start by busting a widely held myth: Co-leadership is suitable for everyone, regardless of work-time model and gender. There are numerous good reasons why co-leadership could be the right way. A leadership tandem sharing a part-time management position can offer double creative power and well-thought-out decisions to the company. Because leadership roles are shared, there are no gaps - as one person is always present.

Tandems that both work full-time have the opportunity to flexibly divide their working time: they can spend 50% of their time as co-leads and the remaining 50% in a specialist position. This offers a unique opportunity to combine two career paths and reconcile different interests.

Co-leadership can also support a planned, strategic generational change. Experienced employees considering retirement can pass on their knowledge during the transition period and prepare the team for the imminent changes. This shared responsibility promotes diversity, facilitates excellent knowledge transfer and also lowers the entry hurdles for managerial positions. However, co-leadership does require a certain 'tandem capability' to be successful.

 

Sharing the sorrow halves the sorrow, sharing the joy doubles the joy?

Since 2018, SAP has become a role model for the co-leadership concept. Our company now advertises all management positions as co-leaderships and has already managed to create about 50 of these tandems. In the coming weeks, we will introduce some of our tandems and provide an insight into how these leaders became co-leads, what challenges they faced at the beginning and how they handle the division of tasks and responsibilities.

 

Keep watching for our upcoming blog post series where we'll be revealing a lot more. The series will kick off next week with our first tandem, Melanie Gomell and Mathias Braje, introducing themselves. So, stay tuned to discover more and bring out your best!

entry.title: Integrate My Inbox link to SAP Flexible Workflow E-Mail notification
entry.content:
We recently came across the problem to integrate a system dependent link to the My Inbox item into SAP Flexible Workflow E-Mail notification. In this blog post, I will describe a possible solution by extending the subsequent CDS View. 

Initial Setup

Let's start from the beginning. We are running a custom SAP Flexible Workflow with two approval steps. Once, there is a new approval task, an E-Mail notification is sent out. Within the E-Mail notification, our requirement is to point to the corresponding item within SAP My Inbox.

For sending E-Mails, we are using Mail Templates which we configured from the "Maintain Email Templates" Fiori App. How this can be configured, is perfectly described in this Blog post. 

In the Mail Template, we use standard HTML link to point to the Inbox. But now, two problems arised. First, the link is system dependent and second, the Mail Template can't be changed in following systems as the mail template will be transported.

 

<a href="https://<system.domain>/sap/bc/ui2/flp#WorkflowTask-displayInbox?allItems=true&/detail/LOCAL_TGW/000000005185/TaskCollection(SAP__Origin='LOCAL_TGW',InstanceID=''{{WorkflowTaskInternalID}}')">inbox</a>

 

Solution Approach

To resolve the system dependency, we had a closer look at the mail template. The template itself doesn't have any information about the system, hence we needed to find a different solution. However, we figured that the template is fed from CDS-View "C_WORKFLOWTASK". So, can we just extend this view?

In the system, we defined an extension to the Inbox and added the required system fields. As you can see from the subsequent code snippet, we took the system URL from table "httpurlloc" and added the client from a session variable.

 

extend view C_Workflowtask with /QPE/E_WF_INBOX 
    association [0..1] to httpurlloc as _http on _http.protocol = 'HTTPS' {
    _http.host as SystemHost,
    _http.port as SystemPort,
    $session.client as SystemClient
}

 

Once activated, we could see the added fields within the data fields of the "Maintain Mail Template" app.

The last step was to use those fields in the link. And then, it works like a charm. 

 

<a href="https://{{SystemHost}}/sap/bc/ui2/flp?sap-client={{SystemClient}}#WorkflowTask-displayInbox&/detail/LOCAL_INBOX/{{WorkflowTaskInternalID}}/TaskCollection(SAP__Origin='LOCAL_INBOX',InstanceID='{{WorkflowTaskInternalID}}')">inbox</a>

 

Conclusion

In this blog post, I presented an approach to extend SAP Flexible Workflow E-Mail notification with a link to My Inbox. However, this approach can also be used for arbitrary other custom fields and also within other mail templates.

entry.title: SAP Stammtisch Melbourne July '24
entry.content:
Melbourne! we are back again for the 3rd time this year. Now in the thick of winter, the perfect time to get out and hang with your SAP buddies.

This time we are going to mix it up a bit and make it a Wednesday in case Thursdays don't work for your regular weekly commitments. The other bonus is that it is $28 Parma & Schooner night! but there are other good deals if you grab an early dinner too.

So far this year (in March & May) we have had a great mix of people at the Melbourne Stammtische, and alway great conversation. I have really enjoyed the relaxed and friendly interactions. It's been brilliant catching up with familiar faces, those I haven't seen for a long time and new ones.

We are now on par with meeting about every two months, with the idea to keep it consistent and provide ample opportunity for people to be able to make it, or just catch up more often. We are not too worried about getting big numbers, but more focused on good interactions. And on that front it has been succeeding, so I'm really looking forward to this next gathering. I hope to see you there, and be sure to spread the word around.

Wednesday 24th July 2024 at 5:30pm at The Bank on Collins 

 

 Ground floor/394 Collins St, Melbourne VIC 3000.

 

When you arrive, if you can't find us you can ask for a booking for the "SAP Regulars Table" 

Ben.

entry.title: 5 Things to Know about Coaching
entry.content:
1. What is coaching?

Think of coaching like forming a professional partnership. It is about sparking deep and creative conversations between you and your coach. It's your vehicle to self-discovery, helping you understand your strengths and pinpoint your developmental goals. Your coach acts as a prompt, partnering with you to efficiently build up your skills and behaviors to make more progress towards these goals. It's about lighting a spark to help you bring out your personal and professional best - like teamwork but with a personal growth twist.

2. Who can benefit from coaching?

Coaching isn't just for a select few; it's for anyone with a thirst for learning and a desire to better themselves. Doesn't matter who you are, as long as you're prepared to roll up your sleeves and dive in. So whether you're looking to conquer a tough challenge, supercharge your growth, welcome big change, or simply unlock the 'you' that you never knew existed, coaching is there for you. It offers a made-to-measure experience that nurtures your strengths, helps you grow, and paves the way for you to shine brighter in your journey!

Here are some examples for you : 

  • Career Transition: Anjali, an accomplished professional, is contemplating a career switch but is unsure how to convey her skills effectively to new industries. A career coach can guide her through this transition, helping to highlight her transferable skills and improve her networking tactics.
  • Leadership Improvement: Rohan has recently become a team leader and is finding it tough to lead effectively. An executive coach can aid him in refining his leadership style, enhancing communication, and fortifying his confidence.
  • Public Speaking: Nitin, a dedicated professional, has a fear of public speaking which hinders his ability to effectively present his ideas. A communications coach can help him gain control over his fear, polish his presentation skills, and improve his confidence, enabling him to communicate effectively in front of an audience.

3. How does coaching work?

Coaching kicks off with a chemistry session, which can be done face-to-face or online, to pinpoint the coachee's goals, hurdles, and what they're aiming for. How often and how these sessions happen depends on what the coachee needs and wants, and they'll tackle tasks that are top priority for them.

The core of coaching is all about boosting self-awareness.

This means the coachee gets to understand their strong points, weak spots, what sets them off, what they value, and how they usually act. It's like a reality check to see where they're at now and where they want to go.

In the world of coaching, being clued in about yourself is key to making the changes you want. Knowing your own thoughts, feelings, and actions, and the effect they have on others, can shine a light on what you might want to work on. This can lead to smarter choices, stronger relationships, and better ways of talking to people.

Coaches are big on using self-awareness to help coachees spot things they might not see and look at things from different angles. They've got a bunch of ways to help coachees become more self-aware, like practical tasks and tests that can nudge them to change their behavior to hit their targets.

Coaching's also about focusing on the good stuff, like handling what life throws at you in a positive way, and talking things out constructively. It's about looking at what's working well and what you need to do to get where you want to be, rather than just zeroing in on the problems. It's meant to get the coachee excited and ready to go after their goals. How long a coach and coachee work together really depends on what the person or business needs and what they prefer.

4. How is it different from other offerings?

  • Leading involves steering and inspiring team members towards a shared vision.
  • Managing is about collaborating with team members to complete daily tasks and achieve organizational objectives.
  • Coaching means fostering personal growth and transformation by tapping into an individual's innate capabilities.
  • Mentoring entails imparting knowledge and insights to nurture and advance someone with less experience.
  • Therapy provides clinical mental health support to assist individuals in managing and alleviating symptoms of mental disorders.

5. Who are coaches?

Credentialed coaches are trained and certified professionals recognized by authoritative bodies like the International Coach Federation (ICF). They've reached specific competence levels, follow a code of ethics, and have completed the required education and experience hours, including passing a thorough exam. To keep their credentials, they must stay current with ongoing professional development, aligning with the latest coaching methods and standards.

Accredited coaches have also completed stringent training and certification, evaluated by institutions like the ICF for their coaching skills, ethical conduct, and dedication to continual learning. Accreditation confirms their ability to deliver top-notch, effective coaching, upholding ethical practices and professional standards. In the coaching field, "accredited" and "credentialed" often mean the same thing, signifying a coach's professional acknowledgment.

Take the next significant step in your personal and professional life with a professional coach. Begin your pioneering journey now! Instead of waiting for the perfect moment, create it. Schedule your initial coaching session to discover your full potential.

entry.title: Getting the Right Coach: How to Ace Your Chemistry Session
entry.content:
A chemistry meeting, typically a complimentary session lasting 30 to 60 minutes, is designed to evaluate the fit between a coach and a prospective client. This step is crucial for establishing trust and a good working relationship. To find the right coach, you may meet with several, compare their approaches, and see which styles might challenge you to grow. Even if you're considering just one coach, it's beneficial to have a chemistry session to gauge your rapport with them.

It is important to talk about your objectives and see if they align with the coach's expertise, as well as to plan for any possible challenges ahead.

 

How to prepare?

Start your preparation by reflecting on both your short-term and long-term goals. Understand what specifically you aim to achieve through coaching. Once you have identified your goals, jot down any questions you might have about the coach, their methods, how they handle challenges, and more.

It's essential to approach the meeting with an open mind, prepared to discuss potential challenges, disagreements, and solutions. Lastly, be ready to share. Your aspirations, strengths, areas for development are all crucial pieces of the puzzle for the coach to understand how best to support you.

 

During the course of the session

Begin your chemistry session with a detailed and engaging introduction that helps build a connection between you and the coach. Convey your goals and what you're seeking in clear terms. This enables the coach to appropriately align their strategies with your objectives. If you harbor any doubts or questions regarding the coaching process, the techniques employed, or how the sessions will be structured, take advantage of this opportunity and ask away.

If you have a particular area of interest or concern, feel free to ask the coach how they plan on addressing this topic.

The chemistry session is an open dialogue - honesty, transparency, and open communication are fundamental. This will help you gauge the coach's flexibility, responsiveness, and whether their approach aligns with your expectations. Dive into open and honest discussions with your potential coach as it aids your decision-making process in choosing the right coach for your unique needs and goals.

 

Arriving at a decision

Choosing the right coach can be greatly influenced by several parameters, key among them are:

  • Rapport: Reflect on your level of comfort during your interactions with the coach. The establishment of a good rapport is crucial for a successful coaching relationship and is often built on mutual respect, shared understanding, and a positive connection. Did the conversation flow easily? Were there shared values or interests that could foster a strong working relationship?
  • Respect: Pay close attention to whether the coach treats you with respect. A good coach values their clients’ opinions and experiences and listens attentively to what they have to say. Does the coach make you feel valued and respected?
  • Feeling Heard: One of the hallmarks of an effective coaching session is a sense of being heard. The coach should be an active listener, taking in your needs, desires, and goals without interruption or judgment. Did you feel understood and acknowledged during your talk?
  • Feeling Safe: Part of creating an effective coaching environment involves feeling safe. This safety refers to both emotional safety, allowing for honest expression of thoughts and feelings, and intellectual safety, enabling the sharing of ideas and goals without fear of humiliation. Did the conversation space feel secure enough for you to be honest?
  • Ability to Speak Openly and Be Vulnerable: Lastly, evaluate whether you felt comfortable enough in the conversation to express your fears, doubts, weaknesses, and aspirations openly. A strong coaching relationship supports vulnerability, encouraging clients to share openly and honestly. Did the coach enable you to express yourself freely and authentically?

 

Remember, selecting a coach is an intensely personal decision. These parameters are designed to ensure that you find a coach who is not only capable and qualified but also aligns with your personality and needs.

entry.title: Wrap up for the first SAP Tech Night (13th June 2024)
entry.content:
Hi Melbourne Community,

Hope everyone had an great time at the first SAP Tech Night or 2024, like i did. 

Thank you Gippy , David and Phil for presenting.

Looking forward to the next one on 22nd Aug 

Would like to take this opportunity to call for speakers and sponsors for the next tech night. 

Planning to follow the similar format of two presentation followed by genenral disucssion.

entry.title: SAP CodeJam HANA ML In 's-Hertogenbosch 2024-06 Recap
entry.content:
In mid-June, we held the Getting Started with Machine Learning using SAP HANA and Python in 's-Hertogenbosch, the Netherlands.

 It was kindly hosted by iTrainee and organized by Mientje and @Jan-WillemK from the Vereniging Nederlandstalige SAP-Gebruikers (VNSG).

It was a mix of some theory...

...and hands-on.

Right after the SAP CodeJam @tbroek , @jpenninkhof , @rakeshjain and truly yours recorded another episode of the HANA Café NL podcast: https://youtu.be/Qm9zEeX6J88?si=XV5FoCvwkDUg6xnX 

I would like to extend special thanks to @OnnoSchoenmaker and @Jan-WillemK for a tour of the city and the introduction to the importance of cheese in Dutch cuisine 🧀

Now, the next stop is Berlin on June 28th hosted by @rofinke : https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-codejam/getting-started-with-machine-learning-using-sap-hana-berlin-de/ev-p/13703853

Would you like to host such an SAP CodeJam? Here are the details: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/quot-getting-started-with-machine-learning-using-sap-hana-quot-as-a-new-sap/ba-p/13574098

entry.title: SAP Community Voice: June 19, 2024, Edition
entry.content:
As announced in the June 5 and May 22 editions of SAP Community Voice, we phased out the email version of the newsletter -- with the very last edition going out on June 19. 

This post includes the highlights from that June 19 edition, and moving forward, the newsletter will live on here in What's New, delivering the hottest content and latest announcements from the SAP Community site.

By subscribing to What's New via the Options menu on that page -- and setting your notification settings to your liking -- you'll still be able to receive the community news, highlights, and other content highlighted in the SAP Community Voice newsletter. You'll also be able to leave kudos and comment on these posts, and your feedback will help us improve.

So while the email version of SAP Community Voice may have come to an end, the newsletter isn't saying goodbye. Instead, we're saying that we hope to see you in the What's New section!

 

Featured Content

 

SAP Sapphire 2024 Highlights About UX, Application Development, and AI

If you're looking for a personal perspective about SAP Sapphire from a member who also happens to be part of the SAP Cloud ERP UX Engineering team, give this post a read. It focuses on UX strategy, SAP Fiori, and SAP S/4HANA Cloud, but also covers AI and other key topics. 

Read the blog post

 

Blog One: Integration Between SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud

The first entry in a five-part series about integrating SAP Datasphere with SAP Analytics Cloud for data harmony, this post provides an overview of SAP Analytics Cloud, explores its advantages, and offers guidance for setting up the data connection for optimal functionality. 

Read the blog post

 

SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA: Trial Systems for Private Cloud and On-Prem

SAP S/4HANA trial systems from SAP Cloud Appliance Library come complete with sample data, test users, and demo guides to get you started. This post shows you how to obtain, use, and extend your trial system, as well as explains what you get with the latest trial systems. 

Read the blog post

 

Our Way into the Clean Core: The Journey of an SAP Software Partner

Clean core remains a hot topic of conversation, and this blog post (from an SAP Community member from a long-standing software partner of SAP) shares the author's experiences (good and bad) with participating in a pilot project for clean-core development on private cloud systems. 

Read the blog post

 

Community News

 

Don't Forget to Subscribe to What's New (and Check Out Our Resources) 

Per the introduction, we may have sent out our last email newsletter…but this isn't the end for SAP Community Voice. What's New is our new home, and from that page, you can locate the Options menu, then select subscribe to keep up on the latest community news and popular content. You can also learn more about SAP Community through our help section, how-to videos, and Tour the SAP Community 2024 tutorial.

 

Find Fun, Educational Events Closer to Home in Our Locations Groups! 

Our events section includes SAP CodeJam, SAP Inside Track, and SAP Community meetups, as well as other online and in-person events, but did you know that several of our location groups have event boards promoting their own local events? For example, São Paulo has an SAP Inside Track scheduled for July 27, and Lingen has a meetup on August 15.

 

Hoodies, Shirts, and Pins -- Oh My! See What's in Our Online Store…

Our supplies seem to be flying off the virtual shelves at the SAP Community online store. We guess that many of you have been paying attention to this promotion and decided to invest in SAP Community gear. Thank you! If you haven't gone shopping in our online store, don't get left out. We still have hoodies, a variety of shirts, and pins. Get yours today and wear your love for SAP Community!

entry.title: Success of SAP STAMMTISCH Kolkata - 22nd June,2024
entry.content:
🎉Celebrating the Success of SAP Stammtisch Kolkata 2024! 🎉

 

 

 

 

 

It was truly a gathering filled with enthusiasm, learning, and meaningful connections among SAP enthusiasts and professionals.

**Highlights from the Event:**
- Engaging Sessions: Participants immersed themselves in insightful discussions on SAP TM, SAP ABAP HR, BTP, Knowledge Graph, and more. The sessions were informative, interactive, and well-received by attendees of all levels of expertise.

- Networking Opportunities: The event provided a platform for attendees to expand their professional networks, exchange ideas, and forge new collaborations within the SAP community in Kolkata.

- Knowledge Sharing: From beginners exploring career opportunities in SAP to seasoned professionals sharing their expertise, the event fostered a culture of continuous learning and skill development.

We extend our heartfelt gratitude to everyone who contributed to the success of this event:
- **Speakers:** Thank you for sharing your expertise and insights.
- **Participants:** Your active participation and enthusiasm made the event lively and impactful.
- **Organizing Team:** Your hard work and dedication ensured a seamless experience for all attendees.
- **Sponsors:** Your support was invaluable in making this event possible.

Stay connected with the SAP Community Kolkata Group to continue enriching your SAP journey. Let's keep the momentum going and look forward to more exciting events ahead!

Thank you once again to everyone who attended and supported the SAP Stammtisch Kolkata 2024. Together, we're building a stronger, more knowledgeable SAP community in Kolkata and beyond.

#SAPKolkataCommunity #SAPCommunity #SuccessStory #SAPStammtisch #KolkataEvents #Networking #SkillBuildin

entry.title: Willkommen/Welcome
entry.content:
Willkommen in der SAP Community Luzern!

Wir freuen uns sehr, euch auf unserer neuen Plattform für die SAP Community Luzern begrüssen zu dürfen! In der wunderschönen Stadt Luzern möchten wir eine dynamische, unterstützende und respektvolle Community aufbauen, die den Austausch von Wissen und Erfahrungen rund um SAP ermöglicht und fördert.

Wer sind wir?

Wir sind eine vielfältige Gruppe von Personen aus dem SAP Umfeld, unterwegs in diversen Rollen (Entwicklung, Architektur, Modulberatung, Studium, usw.) und mit unterschiedlichstem Know-how. Unser Ziel ist es, ein Netzwerk zu schaffen, in welchem wir voneinander lernen und uns gegenseitig weiterbringen können.

Was erwartet dich bei uns?

  • Spannende Events: Wir tauschen uns mehrmals im Jahr bei SAP Stammtisch Treffen zu aktuellen SAP Themen aus und knüpfen spätestens beim lockerem Apéro-Ausklang neue Kontakte. Mit dir gemeinsam können wir vielleicht weitere Events in neuen Formaten realisieren.
  • Wissensaustausch und Network: Auf der Community Seite wollen wir regelmäßig Beiträge zu aktuellen Themen, Best Practices, Tutorials und Erfahrungsberichten teilen und darüber diskutieren. Alle sind eingeladen, mitzumachen, das eigene Wissen zu teilen oder aber akute Fragestellungen zu posten und diese mit der Community zu beantworten.

Wie kannst du mitmachen?

  • Registriere dich: Melde dich auf unserer Plattform als neues Mitglied an, und sei aktiv mit dabei.
  • Teile dein Wissen: Hast du interessante Erfahrungen oder Tipps rund um SAP? Schreibe einen Blogbeitrag, starte eine Diskussion oder halte einen Vortrag bei einem unserer Stammtisch-Events.

Wir freuen uns darauf, dich kennenzulernen!

Deine SAP Community Luzern

 

Welcome to the SAP Community Lucerne!

We are delighted to welcome you to our new platform for the SAP Community Lucerne! In the beautiful city of Lucerne, we aim to build a dynamic, supportive, and respectful community that enables and promotes the exchange of knowledge and experiences around SAP.

Who are we?

We are a diverse group of individuals from the SAP environment, working in various roles (development, architecture, module consulting, studies, etc.) and with a wide range of expertise. Our goal is to create a network where we can learn from each other and help each other grow.

What can you expect from us?

  • Exciting events: Several times a year, we exchange ideas on current SAP topics at SAP Stammtisch meetings and make new contacts at the relaxed aperitif afterwards. Together with you, we might be able to realize additional events in new formats.
  • Knowledge exchange and networking: On the community page, we want to regularly share and discuss posts on current topics, best practices, tutorials, and experiences. Everyone is invited to participate, share their knowledge, or post pressing questions and answer them with the community.

How can you participate?

  • Register: Sign up on our platform as a new member and actively join in.
  • Share your knowledge: Do you have interesting experiences or tips related to SAP? Write a blog post, start a discussion, or give a presentation at one of our Stammtisch events.

We look forward to meeting you!

Your SAP Community Lucerne

entry.title: About SAP S/4HANA Certification.
entry.content:
A certification in S/4 HANA can bring several benefits to your resume, self-esteem, and professional qualifications.

Benefits of the CV:

  1. Professional Appreciation: Certification officially validates your skills and knowledge in S/4 HANA, making your resume more attractive to employers.
  2. Differentiation in the Market: You stand out from the competition with a recognized certification.
  3. Greater Employability: Companies are constantly looking for professionals qualified in S/4 HANA, and having this certification significantly increases your employment opportunities anywhere in the world!

Benefits for Self-Esteem:

  1. Recognition of Competency: Certification means you have advanced knowledge and skills in S/4 HANA, which can increase your professional confidence.
  2. Personal Satisfaction: Completing a challenging certification brings a sense of personal accomplishment and satisfaction, raising your self-esteem.
  3. Motivation: Achieving a certification can serve as motivation to seek even more knowledge and career growth.

Benefits for Professional Qualifications:

  1. Advanced Knowledge: Certification provides a deep understanding of financial processes in S/4 HANA, improving your ability to solve complex problems.
  2. Technological Update: As S/4 HANA is one of SAP's most advanced ERP platforms, certification ensures you are up to date with the latest technologies.
  3. Network: By obtaining certification, you enter a community of certified professionals, which can lead to new networking and collaboration opportunities.
  4. Salary Increase: Certified professionals generally have the potential to receive higher salaries than those without certification.
  5. Career Progression: Certification can open doors to higher positions within your organization or other companies.

Some important steps to prepare for certification:

  1. Have a system with the SAP version you intend to carry out certification with available. In this case, if you don't have one in your company, you can subscribe to a platform such as https://grandesprojetos.com/, which provides SAP courses and offers SAP in different versions. You can subscribe to SAP and access it without purchasing additional courses. However, depending on your level of knowledge, some additional courses may be interesting.
  2. Subscribe to the OpenSAP platform for SAP courses developed by SAP itself. The OpenSAP platform will be discontinued in July 2024, and all its content will be on the SAP Learning website (https://learning.sap.com/opensap-course-migration). This new platform also offers many free SAP courses.
  3. Subscribe to platforms with simulated SAP certification exams; as a suggestion, the website http://www.erpprep.com has good simulations.
  4. Try to do one or two mock tests every day until the exam date. Many questions will be repeated, and you will be increasingly able to answer immediately and without hesitation.

Conclusion:

Investing in an S/4 HANA certification can be a significant step in your career development, providing recognition, knowledge, and opportunities for personal and professional growth.

entry.title: SAP CodeJam HANA ML in Berlin 2024-06 Recap
entry.content:
On the last Friday of June, we held the Getting Started with Machine Learning using SAP HANA and Python in Berlin, Germany.

It was kindly hosted by Zalando and organized by @rofinke 

It was a mix of some theory...

...and hands-on.

It was great to have the product manager responsible for the SAP HANA embedded Machine Learning @ChristophMorgen with us.

 

I would like to extend special thanks to @oliver for organizing and all participants joining SAP Stammtisch Berlin for the day before...


...that I used as well to walk and to see the city...

...including the former Berlin Wall.

Would you like to host such an SAP CodeJam? Here are the details: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/quot-getting-started-with-machine-learning-usin...

entry.title: SAP Enable Now – Create your training materials in the SAP Cloud, for free
entry.content:
SAP Enable Now (formerly known as SAP Workforce Performance Builder and before as Datango) - provide the knowledge your students need to succeed – exactly what, where and when it’s needed.

SAP offers content development software free of charge for SAP University Alliances Program member professors. This includes the authoring tool, “Producer and Instant Producer” which allows you to create your content and makes it easily accessible to your students.
Please note: The usage of SAP Enable Now via the University Alliances Program is only for learning purposes of the students. If a University wants to use Enable Now commercially and/or to support their productive systems they have to buy the product. More information for licensing and purchasing options for SAP Enable Now you can read here.

Your Benefits:
- Create your own training material
- Share this with your students or other universities
- Use this central platform to build training material together with partner universities
- No installation necessary, components will install automatically on-demand
- Always up to date with the newest version, no migration needed on your side
- No basic training required anymore

New Offer for SAP UA members in: SAP Enable Now in the Cloud 
All Components in one Cloud offering https://universityalliances.enable-now.cloud.sap/
C++ components as portables wrapped in JAVA as .jnlp

Interested to use this solution in your classroom?
Please contact us at sapnextgen@sap.com letting us know how do you plan to use the solution, and we will provide you information about the next steps.

Want to learn more about SAP Enable Now? Visit our Info Center to getting started

Check out this video of SAP Enable Now in 2 minutes!  

👍  Thumbs up, if you are you interested in hearing a remote session about SAP Enable Now for academic usage! And we organize the session 👍
